meta.title: ASHIMOI KANKO Takaho Ski Resort
meta.description: All-silver municipal renewal—adult day ticket ¥1,500, pair lift, Fri/Sat night skiing. Numata, Hokkaido · Ashimoi tourism bus.

hero.title:
ASHIMOI KANKO
Takaho Ski Resort
hero.tagline: An all-silver municipal hill—deep-snow family skiing from ¥1,500 a day.

lift.label: Lift
lift.note: 1 silver pair lift · solo ride OK

transit.title:
Ashimoi bus &
Ishikari-Numata Station.
transit.lead: A naming-rights hill operated under Ashimoi Kanko Bus—about 10 minutes by car from JR Ishikari-Numata, with charter and shuttle options from the Eniwa / New Chitose area. ~60 km from Asahikawa into a wide single slope in karamatsu forest.
specs: ~10 min by car from JR Ishikari-Numata
transit.imgAlt: Ashimoi tourism bus on a snowy road to Takaho Ski Resort

primary.title: All-silver renewal
primary.lead: From lift towers to lodge cladding and grooming machines—everything in silver, shedding the "dated municipal hill" image. A modern pair lift with the latest safety gear so beginners and families can ride solo with confidence.
primary.imgAlt: Silver pair lift above a wide slope in karamatsu forest

secondary.title: Strategic ¥1,500 day ticket
secondary.lead: While mega-resorts charge ¥6,000–10,000+ for a day, adults pay ¥1,500 and children ¥750—a municipal loss-leader that keeps a family of four under ¥2,250 for lifts. Fri/Sat night tickets at ¥700 support after-school and after-work laps.

pass.title:
Adults ¥1,500,
children ¥750.
pass.lead: Flat post-renewal pricing—season pass ¥15,000 / ¥7,500, day tickets with night skiing ¥1,500 / ¥750. Fri/Sat night-only ¥700 / ¥350 (16:00–21:00), 3-hour ticket ¥1,000 / ¥500.
pass.noticeBody: Move from PDF and counter-only sales to mobile-first LP, online checkout, and CRM—to maximize ROI on ¥500M+ hardware investment (report ch. 6).
pass.imgAlt: Ticket counter at the silver lodge—value and comfort combined

rental.title:
Silver lodge,
kids' corner & Wi-Fi.
rental.lead: A rebuilt center house with expanded rest space, a kids' area, and free Wi-Fi—municipal comfort that feels resort-grade between runs.
rental.whisper: Light meals at the lodge—pair with a soak at Horoshin Onsen Hotaru-kan on the way home (report ch. 7 cross-sell).
rental.imgAlt: New silver-clad lodge with spacious rest area

yukinko.title:
Fri/Sat nights,
16:00 to 21:00.
yukinko.lead: Night skiing on Fridays and Saturdays from 16:00 to 21:00—¥700 adults / ¥350 children for local after-school and after-work sport under silver-lit slopes.
yukinko.whisper: In season TEL 0164-35-1011 (ski area) · 0164-35-2132 (board of education).
yukinko.imgAlt: Night skiing under silver lifts and slope lights

family.title:
Pair lift,
first snow for families.
family.lead: Solo-friendly modern pair lift, three mostly mellow courses, kids' space in the lodge—positioned between Hokuryo's free rope tow and Horokanai's advanced terrain as a beginner-family sweet spot.
family.noticeBody: Replacing old single chairs and rope tows—the pair lift renewal clears the top deterrent for family visitors from beyond the town (report ch. 2).
family.imgAlt: Parent and child on the pair lift—wide beginner slope below

workation.title:
Day-trip powder
from Asahikawa or Chitose.
workation.lead: ~60 km from Asahikawa or combine Ashimoi Kanko Bus from the New Chitose / Eniwa area—¥1,500 adult day tickets for deep snow without mega-resort crowds (LP concept).

01.title: Ishikari-Numata or bus
01.body: ~10 min by car from JR Ishikari-Numata, or Ashimoi Kanko Bus

02.title: To the silver hill
02.body: Wide single slope in karamatsu forest—virtually no lift lines

03.title: ¥1,500 day ticket
03.body: Day ticket includes night skiing hours on eligible days

04.title: Silver lodge
04.body: Rest, kids' space, and Wi-Fi between runs

05.title: Horoshin Onsen
05.body: Soak at Hotaru-kan after skiing—extend your stay

local.title: Silver renewal × ¥1,500 guide
local.body: Hardware and naming rights are in place—the bottleneck is PDF-heavy town pages. A mobile LP and live conditions make the photogenic silver resort visible at the source (report ch. 5–6).

schools.title: Ashimoi bus × MaaS vision
schools.body: Link naming-rights operator charters and taxis with the hill—JR Ishikari-Numata shuttles and New Chitose-area packages to lower last-mile friction (report ch. 3).

summer.title: Winter CRM → summer cross-sell
summer.body: Horoshin firefly festival, Youtaka lantern festival, fossil museum—use winter visitor data to invite families back in summer for a year-round Numata ecosystem (report ch. 7).

access.note: ~10 min by car from JR Ishikari-Numata · 120 free parking spaces
access.places: 北海道Hokkaido沼田町Numata字高穂325325 Takaho
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at New Chitose Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Eniwa, Numata, and Horoshin Onsen loops
